data.body:
DESIGN SESSION — splitting the workflow-engine remediation epic soupy-spool-revel (6f2d).

RE-TRIAGE of 6f2d (prose predated the close of sibling epics 5fd2 gate + e6d9 engine):
- WS-INTENT-ALIGN: DONE. ISF criterion built+validated under closed story 681b (parent 5fd2); wired in registry.py + prompt files plan_review_ISF/isf_finder/isf_summarizer.md + eval plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml. Built-vs-intent visual-editing reconciliation done in brainstorm ad3f -> restored as ephemeral bpmn-js in e6d9 (closed).
- WS-PROMPTS: SUBSTANTIALLY DONE. Production prompts already live in the git-native prompt library src/rebar/llm/reviewers/*.md (44 files incl. completion_verifier.md, code_quality.md, plan_review_*), via get_prompt + .rebar/prompts overrides + index.json. NOT the docs/experiments artifacts. Residual = prompts-on-NEW-engine, coupled to WS-MIGRATE.
- OPEN: WS-DOC (docs/workflow-engine.md absent), WS-MIGRATE (gate=bespoke orchestrator.py ThreadPoolExecutor; verify_completion=direct completion.py agent call), WS-EVAL-EXISTING (evals exist for plan-review+code-quality, NOT verify_completion/review_ticket/scan_spec), WS-CLEANUP (examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py still present), WS-VISUAL-EVAL (no decision artifact).

BRAINSTORM OUTCOMES (design decisions):
1. The e6d9 engine ALREADY has dynamic map fan-out (map step, iteration-keyed event-log frames, bounded-concurrent map_fanout.py). Pydantic AI runtime is ALREADY shared (two runner seams: Runner/PydanticAIRunner for the gate; AgentStepRunner for engine prompt steps). So 'one substrate' is mostly true at runtime; what's duplicated is orchestration STYLE.
2. Pass-2 verify = ONE aggregate call (no N-fan-out). Only real fan-out is Pass-1 finders.
3. THE decisive split (grounded in Argo/Step Functions/dbt/LangGraph/Airflow/Dagster/Temporal prior art): CONDITIONAL OVERLAY = first-class DECLARATIVE construct (when/enabled predicate over recorded inputs; resolved set JOURNALED at run-start for replay safety; Argo when:/dbt enabled). ADAPTIVE BATCHING = NOT first-class — no engine does it, and it would break the interpreter's replay invariant (batch composition depends on runtime token-estimate + which call hit a context-limit => control state is a recorded DECISION-TREE, not a function of outputs). So: a THIN batch step declaring {group,usd_budget,model_ladder} that DELEGATES adaptive packing/fallback/escalation/shed/checkpoint to a batch-RUNNER which journals plan+fallback as OPAQUE events the interpreter stores but never interprets. The 'biggest blocker' (Pass-1 batching) is therefore NOT added to the workflow system — it stays runner-level.
4. Other 4-pass concepts FIT existing kinds: DET P1-P8 = uses; per-pass model-tiering = model: attr; find->verify->decide->coach = ordinary sequencing; coverage accounting + HMAC sign = uses steps. Budget-cap/checkpoint/cap-overflow stay runner-level inside the batch step.
5. COMPLETION VERIFIER fits EXISTING concepts, simpler than plan-review: uses(child-closure+signature precheck) -> prompt(agentic, one call) -> uses(reconcile) -> uses(sign). NO new constructs. The walking-skeleton migration.
6. PARALLEL VALIDATION = PLANNED-TRACE parity, NOT result parity (LLM is variable). Compare the ordered intended-trace [DET-check-id | (criterion/prompt-id, INTENDED model, call-mode agent|1shot) | deterministic-step-id], PRE-escalation, OFFLINE via FakeRunner/FakeAgentRunner (no live calls) over a diverse scenario corpus (CI-able). Capture INTENDED model (fake echoes model=None).
7. Mock substrate already exists: FakeRunner (runner.py, config.runner='fake', no model/network) + FakeAgentRunner (executor.py, --dry-run). Enables the offline parity harness.

TICKETS CREATED:
- Epic A sage-votary-logic (5766-fe6f-83d0-4252): engine-only — overlay construct + delegating batch step + throwaway demo + visual editing for new constructs. Children: A1 overlay (87d2, ready), A2 batch (9df3, ready), A3 demo (9706, blocked by A1+A2), A4 visual-editing (9f5b, blocked by A1+A2).
- Epic B peak-fir-hilt (72d5-79ec-9e2e-4a2b): migrate plan-review + completion-verifier onto the engine; planned-trace parity; remove sample stubs. depends_on A.
- Wiring: B depends_on A; 6f2d depends_on B; A & B relate_to 6f2d. 6f2d retains WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING + WS-VISUAL-EVAL.
NEXT: implement epic A on a new branch (A1+A2 first, then A3+A4), code-review subagent before each close, PR to main.
